title: Mechanical Insulation Installation Incentive Act of 2025
short_title: Mechanical Insulation Installation Incentive Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill creates a new tax credit to help people save money when they add insulation to their heating and cooling systems. People can get ten percent of their money back for paying workers to install this special insulation. The systems must be at least one year old and meet energy rules to qualify. This tax credit will be available from 2026 to 2028.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Ways and Means.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Mechanical Insulation Installation Incentive Act of twenty twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2 Labor Costs of Installing Mechanical Insulation Property
sections.text: This section changes the tax code to create a new tax credit. The credit helps people who pay workers to install insulation on mechanical systems like heating and cooling equipment. Here is how the credit works. People can get a tax credit equal to ten percent of what they pay workers to install mechanical insulation. The credit only applies to labor costs, which means the money paid to workers for their time and work. It does not include the cost of buying the insulation materials themselves. To qualify for this credit, several things must be true. The mechanical system being insulated must be at least one year old before the new insulation is added. The system must be located in the United States and must be the type of equipment that businesses can claim depreciation on for tax purposes. The insulation work must meet the minimum standards set by Reference Standard ninety point one, which is an energy efficiency guideline. The new insulation must actually reduce energy loss from the mechanical system. The term mechanical insulation property includes the insulation materials themselves plus any covering materials and accessories that are installed along with the insulation. This can include insulation that is put together at a different location before being installed. This new tax credit becomes part of the general business credit that companies can use to reduce their taxes. However, if someone claims this credit, they cannot also deduct the full amount of labor costs as a business expense. They must reduce their deduction by the amount of the credit they received. If the credit is bigger than what they could deduct anyway, they must also reduce the amount they add to their capital account. This credit will only be available for labor costs paid between January first twenty twenty six and December thirty first twenty twenty eight.
